mappings.rationaleAI: The cultural history of food covers the Columbian Exchange introducing New World crops, food preservation and industrialization, and the modern food industry, spanning agricultural history and food processing.

Coffee covers its origins in Ethiopia, the colonial plantation economy, physiological effects of caffeine, and coffeehouse culture, directly relevant to production of major field crops and nonalcoholic beverage technology.

The Columbian Exchange covers the transfer of crops (potatoes, maize, tomatoes) and livestock between hemispheres, directly relevant to the history of agriculture, major crops, and new food source development.
Fungi covers mycorrhizal symbiosis with plant roots (relevant to crop farming), fungal pathogens like wheat rust (pest control), and fermentation applications (food/beverage production).
Louis Pasteur developed pasteurisation for food preservation and studied fermentation, directly relevant to food preservation through high-temperature methods and food quality control.
Parasitism covers parasitic diseases affecting livestock and the ecological significance for pest control, relevant to crop and livestock disease control.
Virgil's Georgics is a didactic poem on agriculture covering crops, trees/vines, livestock, and beekeeping, directly mirroring the outline's categories of crop and livestock farming.
The 1816 Year Without a Summer covers volcanic climate forcing causing crop failures and famine, relevant to the history of agriculture and farming in relation to weather.
Genetic engineering covers GMOs in agriculture, directly relevant to plant propagation techniques and utilization of new food sources.

Pollination covers co-evolutionary relationships between flowering plants and pollinators, pollinator decline, and the economic value to global food production, directly relevant to plant propagation and horticultural crops.
Microbiology covers applications in food production (fermentation), public health, and the relationship between microorganisms and food safety, relevant to food processing and preservation.
